description: bun add of latest deps for a Next.js 15 repo resolves typescript@7.0.2, eslint@10, and eslint-config-next@16, which peer-conflict with Next 15 (four 'incorrect peer dependency' warnings on install)
resolution: Explicitly pin the toolchain majors after the bulk install: bun add -d typescript@5 eslint@9 eslint-config-next@15. Do not rely on unpinned 'latest' for TS/ESLint on a Next 15 project.

content: shadcn/ui CLI cannot be initialized non-interactively in this repo: 'bunx shadcn add ... --yes' still prompts to create components.json and hangs the agent. Hand-write components.json first (style new-york, rsc true, css src/app/globals.css, aliases @/components + @/lib/utils), then 'bunx --bun shadcn@latest add <components> --yes --overwrite' runs clean.

title: Health endpoint kept dependency-free and verified at real runtime
rationale: Railway's healthcheck on the web service runs before Postgres/Redis plugins are necessarily reachable, so /api/health must not import db/redis or it will fail the deploy gate on a cold project. Route is 'export const dynamic = force-dynamic' with a plain NextResponse.json. Also: a passing handler-level bun test does NOT prove the deploy gate works - the handler test bypasses Next's route resolution. Smoke-tested by running 'bun run start' and curling the endpoint (HTTP 200, {status:ok,uptime,timestamp}). Do this for any endpoint an external orchestrator polls.

description: Local integration verification on this Mac hits two environment traps: (1) Docker Desktop is uninstalled but ~/.docker/config.json still names credsStore 'docker-credential-desktop', so any 'docker run' that pulls an image dies with 'error getting credentials'. (2) There is no native Postgres (no psql binary) and the container runtime is colima, whose VM is pre-provisioned but normally Stopped.
resolution: Point DOCKER_CONFIG at a temp dir holding an empty {} config.json rather than editing the user's ~/.docker/config.json; because that also discards docker context selection, set DOCKER_HOST=unix://$HOME/.colima/default/docker.sock explicitly alongside it. Run 'colima start' first. Use the postgres:16 container and run psql via 'docker exec'. Restore prior machine state afterward: remove container+image, 'colima stop' (it was Stopped before).

content: package.json intentionally uses bare major ranges (next '15', eslint '9', typescript '5', eslint-config-next '15') while bun.lock pins exact resolutions: next 15.5.21, react 19.2.8, typescript 5.9.3, eslint 9.39.5, eslint-config-next 15.5.21, tailwindcss 4.3.3. The guard against the previously-seen bad resolution (typescript@7 / eslint@10 / eslint-config-next@16, which peer-conflict with Next 15) is the LOCKFILE, not the manifest — so never 'bun install' bare or bump the manifest expecting protection. 'bun install --frozen-lockfile' installs the pinned set, fails loudly on drift, and leaves bun.lock clean in git status. Verified 2026-07-21: frozen install exit 0, all six versions matched, lockfile unmodified. Corollary: a canonical checkout with no node_modules makes tsc/eslint missing and 4 tests fail on unresolved imports — that is an environment gap, not a code defect.

description: Quality gates (bun test/lint/typecheck) failed in a fresh overstory git worktree with 'Cannot find package zod' — git worktrees do NOT share node_modules with the canonical repo, and the canonical repo had none installed either.
resolution: Run 'bun install --frozen-lockfile' in the worktree before running gates; node_modules is gitignored so it never enters the commit/scope. bun.lock is present in-tree so the frozen install is deterministic.

description: In an overstory worktree, bun test/typecheck/lint fail with 'Cannot find package zod/next' because the worktree has NO node_modules (none up the tree, incl. canonical root). Easy to misread as a bracket-path [id] resolution bug; it is not — a non-bracket test fails identically.
resolution: Run 'bun install' in the worktree root once before quality gates (bun.lock present; ~4s, lockfile unchanged). Bracketed [id] route dirs resolve fine once node_modules exists.
